title: Executive Order 8751—Establishing the Division of Defense Aid Reports in the Office For Emergency Management of the Executive Office of the President

flags.justification: The Executive Officer's functions (Sec. 2) are administrative and coordinative — a central clearance channel, records-keeping, reporting — not substantive policy-making authority.
The Executive Officer's duties are specifically enumerated (Sec. 2a-d), and records/accounts require Bureau of the Budget approval — a defined structure.
Single purpose: Lend-Lease administration and reporting.
An internal administrative/reporting body creates no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

Authority is tied to 'the national emergency as declared by the President on September 8, 1939' and administers the Lend-Lease Act, with no reauthorization or review mechanism despite being emergency-contingent.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No direct third-party-facing compliance regime is created by this internal administrative body.
Routine administrative-support machinery for the larger Lend-Lease program; not itself a novel authority claim.
No other agency's mandate is redirected; this creates a new internal division within the Office for Emergency Management.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
